page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is a general-purpose server instance powered by the ARMv8 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores using the ARM Neoverse-N1 model running at 3.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the average tier for multi-core operations and memory throughput. The stress-ng multi-core test yields 28983.31, and the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 9311.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1129.87 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 129.41 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1078.0 and a weak single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 8.61 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations also show limitations, with a weak PassMark CPU encryption score of 2588.14.

The t2a-standard-16 offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated ARM cores rather than hyperthreaded x86 cores. The absence of local storage and weak single-core execution speed are notable tradeoffs. This instance is suitable for scale-out workloads, static web serving, database operations, and software compilation. It is less suitable for heavy cryptographic tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies on network-attached storage. Benchmark results show average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28983.31 and a static web serving throughput of 18.26 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and cryptographic speeds are weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1078.0 and a low PassMark encryption score. The instance provides a resource-dense, cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded or cryptographically intensive applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based server with 16 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based server featuring 16 dedicated cores and 64 GB RAM for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance delivering dedicated physical cores and balanced memory allocation for multi-threaded scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Average multi-core performance suitable for database operations and web serving
Weak single-core and cryptographic benchmark results
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
